item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk Team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

Reading this article immediately reminded me of the very similar SCP-1659, which is another anomaly that randomly inducts individuals into an anomalous organization with strange goals. A crosslink to that could be added.

Description: SCP-2390 designates a sudden mental affliction which causes affected persons to believe that they are members of a secret organization (hereby known as SCP-2390-1-XX) dedicated to the regulation of society in regards to a specific product, activity, or trend.

To:

Description: SCP-2390 designates a sudden mental affliction which causes affected persons to believe that they are members of a secret organization (hereby known as SCP-2390-1-XX) dedicated to the regulation of society in regards to a specific product, activity, or trend.1

If anyone has any other idea, feel free to suggest them below.

Footnotes
1. Investigation of a connection between SCP-2390 and SCP-1659 is being investigated, due to similarity of function.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Typo there; you wrote SCP-2930-1-AA. Similar typos around that area.
For a moment I actually thought you actually meant SCP-2930 and went to take a look at 2930 page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea is simple but brilliant, and very well realised. The touches of humour are the icing on the cake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the Foundation itself a SCP-2390 instance?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought this would end up being something created or related to AWCY, as it just seemed like something they would produce. Regardless, I still loved it. Imagining the lengths these groups would go to to contain things left a smile on my face. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the ruling council a 2390 instance? It's the obvious implication.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
sounds more like Canadian geese
+ Did I do good miss?
- Title for hiding text
[Your text]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought this was pretty clever, and well written.

I tend not to enjoy SCPs that involve some kind of convoluted and specific mental process, but honestly, this worked for me. It acknowledged the inherent ridiculousness of the process and didn't take it too seriously, which helped.

"When confronted with this repeatedly, members of SCP-2390-1 will become increasingly agitated, insisting that the true nature of its focus is dangerous, finally resulting in a sudden exclamation on the part of the SCP-2390-1 member, accompanied by a weak compulsion being experienced by subjects within a 10 meter radius to research the focus of the group, sufficiently strong to halt questioning. █" <— This set of events in particular did actually stand out as overly contrived, but that was the only one. If you either made the events simpler or made it into more than one sentence, might be better.

(Also, you did a nice thing with the exposition here, that also helped the article- slipping some small details about how the thing plays out into footnotes to the interview. I liked having them, but loading them in the description would have been too much, so the way you did it was good.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I get the feeling that in this SCP's canon, the O5 Council is a SCP-2390 instance that is fixated on "anomalies", which just happened to gain a significant amount of power.

item.title: Re: Illuminati foreshadowing?
item.content:encoded: Let's have some off-page Word of God from the author, courtesy of the announcements forum.

item.title: Re: Illuminati foreshadowing?
item.content:encoded:
This is a collaborative creative writing site that is home to laser butt disease, the butt ghost, and a substantial amount of non-butt-related shenanigans, among our more serious works.

Putting a "professional-looking" signature-esque signoff comes off as a bit silly when used in these forums, given that no one else does so and it's essentially unnecessary.

Edit: goddamnit comma

item.title: Re: Illuminati foreshadowing?
item.content:encoded: I know, it just gives it a more professional look to me. If it's just annoying though, I'll stop.

item.title: Re: Illuminati foreshadowing?
item.content:encoded: BTW: Didn't see footnote number five until after I posted that. Makes me wonder even more… am I onto something here?

item.title: Re: Illuminati foreshadowing?
item.content:encoded: Just a heads up, your username automatically appears over every post you make, so there's no need to sign your comments. It's an open discussion forum, not an email chain. ;)

item.title: Illuminati foreshadowing?
item.content:encoded:
Cut me some slack here, I know it's far-fetched, but to me it makes sense: Could this possibly be a foreshadowing of the Illuminati? Since all of their conspiracies revolve around trends and what's running the trends?

If you're just going "No, no, no. Just no," I'll handplant a facepalm. Just say the word.

-OA

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I meant the fact that it caused the disappearance of things like the scrunchie was funny to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Notice how it doesn't only say trend.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
there's a pretty clear reason for the tics
Simple logic
  • The focus of an SCP-2390 instance is believed by its members to be a threat to the world whose actions must be opposed at all costs.
  • The group considers itself to be the only individuals capable of opposing SCP-2390, and are compelled to do so.
  • The focus of SCP-2390-AA is SCP-2390.
  • SCP-2390-AA must oppose all actions made by instances of SCP-2390.
  • SCP-2390-AA is an instance of SCP-2390.
  • Any attempt made by SCP-2390-AA to oppose SCP-2390 must be opposed by SCP-2390-AA.
  • Any attempt made by SCP-2390-AA to oppose the attempts of SCP-2390-AA to oppose SCP-2390 must be opposed by SCP-2390-AA.
  • Any attempt made by SCP-2390-AA to oppose the attempts of SCP-2390-AA to oppose the attempts of SCP-2390-AA to oppose SCP-2390 must be opposed by SCP-2390-AA.
  • Any attempt…
  • …
  • *system error*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It literally says trend in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2390 designates a sudden mental affliction which causes affected persons to believe that they are members of a secret organization (hereby known as SCP-2390-1-XX) dedicated to the regulation of society in regards to a specific product, activity, or trend.

Right there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They're not concealing trends, they're a fucking Foundation parody. They don't try to stop "trends," they try to stop random shit. They might decide blankets warrant containment. They might decide that some random dude warrants containment. The point is that they're grabbing up random stuff that isn't anomalous. I'm not sure where you're getting "trends" from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Concealing trends is basically removing them from the public eye.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They don't get rid of trends. They secure things that don't make sense, in order to contain them and protect humanity. Get it?

Also, Frozen isn't a "trend," it's an animated film.

Also also, you take that back. Frozen is amazing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So a group that gets rid of trends. Well played.

+1 (I hope they get rid of Frozen)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get the bit about facial tics. Besides that, I thought it was fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Liked it in drafts, still like it! Yay!

Edit: It still mentions the incident in the footnotes. Wasn't sure if that was intended or not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Removed the expungement and polished it up slightly. I probably should have waited for feedback on the changes, but my enthusiasm comes in short bursts over periods of up to several months and I needed to do something with it.
